strategy:
Federation of Sol — Opening Strategy

Summary:
Sol is the strongest infantry faction in the game and your whole opening should lean on that. You want to expand wide and fast, flip planets aggressively, and use your superior ground game to make every contested planet a bad trade for your neighbors. Prioritize tempo over tall economy in the first two rounds.

Faction read:
Your commodity-to-trade-good flexibility (Orbital Drop, free infantry from the Spec Ops II tech and the Sol flagship) means you can throw ground forces at planets your opponents cannot cheaply defend. Lean into Politics/Leadership early to bank command tokens and keep your tactic pool deep so you can out-activate the table.

Opening priorities:
- Grab both your home-adjacent resource planets on Round 1 — do not leave influence on the table.
- Contest the nearest shared system before your neighbor commits to it; your infantry win the ground fight.
- Take an early Strategy card that fills your tactic pool (Leadership or Politics) so you can expand to 3-4 systems by end of Round 2.

Round one plan:
1. Strategic action first to bank command tokens if you take Leadership.
2. Tactical action into your richest adjacent system, drop infantry, build a Space Dock if the system has 2+ planets.
3. Second tactical action into the shared/contested system to plant a flag before your neighbor can.
4. End the round with units positioned to threaten Mecatol Rex on Round 2 rather than committing to it now.

Tech path:
- Neural Motivator (more action cards / card draw tempo)
- Spec Ops II (free infantry — your bread and butter)
- Carrier II or Antimass Deflectors depending on wormhole pressure

Expansion targets:
- Your two home-adjacent planets
- The nearest 2-planet shared system toward the center
- A wormhole-adjacent system if it lets you flank a neighbor

Risks:
- Over-committing to Mecatol Rex on Round 1 and getting counter-attacked while tapped out.
- Spreading ground forces so thin that a single PDS-backed counter retakes a planet.

Mistakes to avoid:
- Hoarding command tokens you should be spending to expand.
- Building War Suns or other expensive units before you have the economy to sustain them.
